intro: The clearest difference between Sentry and Bugsnag is lock-in — Sentry has low lock-in (Low — open-source, self-hosted option, OTel-compatible), whereas Bugsnag has medium lock-in (Error monitoring, data exportable). Sentry is described as open-source error tracking and performance monitoring for web, mobile, and backend with distributed tracing and session replay, and Bugsnag is error monitoring platform for web, mobile, and server apps with stability scores, grouping, and release tracking. Sentry fits error tracking and crash reporting for any platform; Bugsnag fits mobile. On the strengths side, Sentry is cited for error tracking and crash reporting for any platform, while Bugsnag is cited for mobile and web apps in production. The honest trade-off: Sentry is the wrong call for full APM with infrastructure monitoring; Bugsnag is the wrong call for pre-launch, Simple projects; Sentry users flag full APM with infrastructure monitoring, and Bugsnag users flag Pre-launch/beta apps. Choosing between them should come down to the constraint that actually binds your workflow, not surface-level overlap.
quick_take: Sentry is for error tracking and crash reporting; Bugsnag is for mobile; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Sentry if your use case is error tracking and crash reporting for any platform, and low lock-in is acceptable in exchange for its fit.
verdict_b: Choose Bugsnag if your use case is mobile, and medium lock-in is acceptable in exchange for its fit.

faq.a: Both Sentry and Bugsnag use a freemium model with a free tier, so raw pricing is close on paper. The real cost difference depends on volume, seats, and which paid features you actually need. Check current pricing on each vendor's page before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Sentry to Bugsnag?
faq.a: Migrating from Sentry to Bugsnag is feasible when their scopes overlap, but the work is in re-mapping configuration, data models, and integrations. Sentry has low lock-in, so exporting state is typically straightforward. Plan for a dual-run period so you can validate parity before decommissioning.

Sentry and Bugsnag score the same on developer experience (5/5 each) in our data. Choose based on which workflow, docs style, and CLI ergonomics feel closer to your team's preferences. Trial both in a small project before committing.

faq.q: Is Bugsnag a good alternative to Sentry?
faq.a: Bugsnag is a reasonable alternative to Sentry when Bugsnag fits mobile. If your use case matches Sentry's sweet spot — error tracking and crash reporting for any platform — staying with Sentry is probably safer. Sentry itself is not meant for full APM with infrastructure monitoring, which is often where users start shopping for alternatives.
